2605.25856.title: Explaining Too Much? Understanding How Large Language Model Reasoning Traces Influence Performance and Metacognition
2605.25856.paper_summary: Large Language Model interfaces are increasingly verbose, exposing intermediate reasoning traces alongside final answers. Traces are framed as transparency mechanisms, yet it is unclear how people use them to solve problems. We report a preregistered between-subjects study (N = 559) in which participants solved ten LSAT-style reasoning problems under one of three conditions: an Answer-only baseline, a Full-trace revealed before the answer, and a Summary-trace presented alongside the answer. Summaries preserved task performance at the no-trace baseline while significantly elevating trust and hedonic appeal, establishing that trace exposure shifts subjective appraisal of the interaction without bringing performance benefits. Under an open-weight reasoning model exposing verbose intermediate output, full traces additionally impaired performance relative to the answer-only baseline. Across all conditions, participants substantially overestimated their performance, and no trace format supported calibrated self-evaluation. Further analysis indicates that hedonic appeal, not trust, carries the indirect path to overestimation, consistent with a processing-fluency account. Reasoning traces are best understood as user-facing interface artifacts rather than transparent windows into model cognition, and calibration is unlikely to emerge from the traces themselves and may best be scaffolded by interactions that elicit users' own reasoning first.
2605.25856.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型介面越來越冗長，除了最終答案外，還顯示了中間推理的痕跡。這些痕跡被視為透明性機制，但尚不清楚人們如何利用它們來解決問題。我們報告了一項預註冊的被試間研究（N = 559），參與者在三種條件之一下解決了十個類似LSAT的推理問題：僅答案的基線、在答案之前揭示的完整痕跡，以及與答案一起呈現的摘要痕跡。摘要在無痕跡基線下保持了任務表現，同時顯著提高了信任感和享樂吸引力，確立了痕跡曝光改變互動的主觀評價，而不帶來性能上的好處。在一個揭示冗長中間輸出的開放權重推理模型下，完整痕跡相對於僅答案的基線還進一步損害了表現。在所有條件下，參與者都大幅高估了自己的表現，且沒有任何痕跡格式支持精確的自我評估。進一步分析表明，享樂吸引力而非信任感，通過間接途徑導致高估，這與處理流暢度的解釋一致。推理痕跡最好被理解為面向用戶的介面工件，而不是模型認知的透明窗口，且從痕跡本身不太可能產生校準，可能最好通過引發用戶自身推理的互動來進行支撐。
2605.25856.author: Daniela Fernandes et.al.
2605.25856.authors: Daniela Fernandes, Daniel Buschek, Lev Tankelevitch, Thomas Kosch, Robin Welsch
